question_text: A metal sphere has a mass of 2.7 kg. It has a radius of 10 cm (volume of sphere = 4/3 × π × r³). Calculate the density of the metal sphere. (Use π = 3.14 and give your answer to 2 decimal places.)
explanation: First calculate the volume using the sphere formula, then convert to m³ (divide by 1,000,000). Finally, divide mass by volume to find density. This tests understanding of formulas and unit conversion.
